news.headline: Genuine superstar Kane produces his biggest England moment
news.desc: Harry Kane's England career is filled with great moments - his World Cup rescue act against DR Congo in Atlanta is his greatest, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

news.headline: USA march on but will Balogun red card prove costly?
news.desc: Mauricio Pochettino's USA side set up a World Cup last-16 meeting with Belgium, but how costly will forward Folarin Balogun's red card prove?

news.headline: Latest ever winner, comeback and controversy - Senegal's remarkable exit
news.desc: Late drama, controversy, history and heartbreak - the World Cup last-32 tie between Belgium and Senegal had everything.

news.headline: England into last 16 - what is their path to the final?
news.desc: BBC Sport takes a look at what England will need to do to reach the final after progressing the last 16

news.headline: Tuchel urges parents to let kids stay up for England game
news.desc: England manager Thomas Tuchel urges parents to let their children stay up to watch England take on Mexico in the last 16 of the World Cup.

news.headline: Why did England struggle against DR Congo - and what can they learn?
news.desc: A tactical analysis of England's victory over DR Congo - and what it might teach them for their next World Cup match against Mexico.

news.headline: 'Why not take advantage?' - did hydration breaks spark England comeback?
news.desc: England came from behind to beat DR Congo and reach the last 16 of the World Cup, but did the momentum noticeably swing their way after the hydration breaks?

news.headline: What you need to know about England's last-16 opponents Mexico
news.desc: England will face Mexico in the World Cup round of 16. Here is what you need to know about them.

news.headline: Has football fever taken hold in the US?
news.desc: With the World Cup now three weeks old, BBC Sport's reporters in the US look at how their perceptions have changed.

news.headline: England sneak through - but who had a nightmare start? Player ratings
news.desc: BBC Sport England reporter Alex Howell rates the players after the World Cup comeback win over DR Congo - plus have your say.

news.headline: Lopes will relish Messi duel, says Cape Verde defender's mother
news.desc: The prospect of facing World Cup record goalscorer Lionel Messi in a knockout game would strike fear into most defenders, but not Roberto Lopes, according to the Cape Verde defender's mother.

news.headline: United, free and brilliant - can anyone stop France masterclass?
news.desc: France boss Didier Deschamps urges caution despite watching his side dismantle Sweden to leave Les Bleus fans dreaming of winning the World Cup.
